nameMeanings.name: Bean
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname for a pleasant person, from Middle English bēne ‘friendly, amiable’.

English: metonymic occupational name for a grower or seller of beans, from Middle English bene ‘bean’ (Old English bēan ‘beans’, a collective singular). The broad bean, Vicia faba, was a staple food in Europe in the Middle Ages. The green bean, Phaseolus vulgaris, came from South America and was not introduced to Europe until the late 16th century. The word bene was commonly used to denote something of little worth, and occasionally it may have been applied as a nickname for someone considered insignificant.

English: possibly a habitational or topographic name. Redmonds, Dictionary of Yorkshire Surnames, cites Adam del Bene of Harrogate (1351) as evidence to suggest that in the Harrogate area, where the Yorkshire name later proliferated, it may have been derived from a place where beans grew.

variants.name: Beaner
meanings: English: occupational name for a grower or seller of beans, from an agent derivative of Old English bēan ‘beans’ (see Bean ). This surname has apparently died out in Britain.
Probably also an Americanized form of German   Biener   or some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Beans
meanings: English: variant of Bean , with post-medieval excrescent -s.
Probably an Americanized form Americanized form of German or Swiss German   Bins   or   Binz  , or of some other similar (like-sounding) German surname.

variants.name: Behan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Conan, Aileen.
Irish (southern): Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Beachain ‘descendant of Beachán’, a personal name from a diminutive of beach ‘bee’.
Irish (southern): perhaps from Ó Beathain ‘descendant of Beathán’, a personal name based on a diminutive of beatha ‘life’. Compare Bean 5 and McBeth .

variants.name: Beene
meanings: Americanized form of German   Bien  , Dutch   Been  , or of some other similar (like-sounding) German or Dutch surname.

variants.name: Benn
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Ben(ne), which is in part a short form of Benet (see Benedict ).
North German: from a short form of the personal name   Bernhard  .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .
